title: arabis nemorensis subsp. planisiliqua
intro: Arabis nemorensis subsp. planisiliqua, commonly known as woodland rock-cress or woodland rockcress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as an antioxidant, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, Arabis nemorensis subsp. planisiliqua is typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to support health and well-being.
